text: The Desert Fathers of fourth-century Egypt built their withdrawal theology around a single Greek word: hesychia, meaning stillness or interior quiet. Abba Moses taught that a monk who leaves his cell for the crowd loses not just time but a specific cognitive capacity — the ability to hear the 'logismoi,' the subtle interior thoughts that, when attended to, reveal the roots of compulsive behavior. This is strikingly close to modern metacognitive research: the default mode network, active in quiet solitude, performs the self-referential processing that enables pattern recognition about one's own mental habits. The Fathers were mapping, in spiritual language, real neurological territory.
question: What capacity did Abba Moses warn a monk loses by leaving his cell for the crowd?

options.text: The ability to memorize scripture accurately
The ability to hear subtle interior thoughts revealing roots of compulsive behavior
